source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in the rhombus $ABCD$, the diagonals $AC$ and $BD$ intersect at point $O$, and $H$ is the midpoint of side $AD$. If the perimeter of the rhombus $ABCD$ is $24$, what is the length of $OH$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since in the rhombus $ABCD$, the perimeter is 24, \\
it follows that $OA=OC$, $CD=24\div 4=6$, \\
since $H$ is the midpoint of side $AD$, \\
it follows that $OH=\frac{1}{2}CD=\boxed{3}$.

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: In the rhombus $ABCD$, the diagonals $AC$ and $BD$ intersect at point $O$, and $H$ is the midpoint of side $AD$. If the perimeter of the rhombus $ABCD$ is $24$, what is the length of $OH$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text: Since the perimeter of rhombus $ABCD$ is $24$, each side has length $24 \div 4 = 6$. In triangle $ACD$, $O$ is the midpoint of $AC$ and $H$ is the midpoint of $AD$, so $OH = \frac{1}{2}CD = 3$.
